applicability.notes: Periodic Inspection per Section 8.2.4. Performed at 1 to 12-month intervals depending on usage and manufacturer recommendations. Includes all Frequent Inspection items plus detailed examination of structural components, mechanical systems, and comprehensive operational testing. Load test and dielectric test may be performed separately using dedicated templates.

severity.label: Unsafe / Out of Service - Tag out equipment

wear_level.label: Minor - Light wear, no action needed
Requires Replacement - Replace before next inspection

inputs.label: Include Load Test in this inspection?
inputs.help_text: Check YES if load test was performed as part of this periodic inspection, or NO if performed separately

inputs.label: Include Dielectric Test in this inspection (for insulated units)?
inputs.help_text: For insulated units: Check YES if dielectric test was performed as part of this inspection, or NO if performed separately

fields.label: Inspection Interval
fields.help_text: Select the maintenance schedule interval for this equipment

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

fields.label: Manufacturer's operation and maintenance manual reviewed
fields.help_text: Review manufacturer's specific periodic inspection requirements

fields.label: Previous inspection records and service history reviewed
fields.help_text: Check for recurring issues, recent repairs, or modifications

fields.label: Any modifications, repairs, or alterations since last inspection reviewed and documented
fields.help_text: Verify all modifications are documented and approved per Section 7.11

fields.label: Manufacturer's parts list and inspection requirements reviewed
fields.help_text: Ensure you have correct specifications for this model

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Thoroughly inspect all structural members for cracks, deformation, corrosion, and damage
Pay special attention to high-stress areas and welded joints
Check for any signs of metal fatigue or stress fractures
Look for evidence of overloading or impact damage
Check alignment of boom sections during extension/retraction
Inspect platform attachment points for cracks or elongated holes
Check outrigger beam alignment and pad condition
Document exact location and extent of any structural defects found

fields.label: Boom Base Section
fields.help_text: Check for cracks, bends, corrosion, stress fractures

fields.label: Boom Fly Section (including platform attachment)
fields.help_text: Inspect outermost section and platform mounting points

fields.label: Boom Wear Pads and Slides
fields.help_text: Check wear on telescoping section slides

fields.label: Turret Structure and Frame
fields.help_text: Complete inspection of rotating turret structure

fields.label: Turret Bearing and Race
fields.help_text: Check bearing for wear, smooth rotation, no grinding

fields.label: Pedestal and Mounting Structure
fields.help_text: Critical mounting - inspect for cracks, deformation

fields.label: Pedestal Mounting Bolts to Chassis
fields.help_text: Critical bolts - check torque and condition

fields.label: Platform Floor Condition
fields.help_text: Check for holes, cracks, excessive wear

fields.label: Outrigger/Stabilizer Structure
fields.help_text: Inspect all outrigger beams for straightness, cracks

fields.label: Outrigger Pads
fields.help_text: Check pads for cracks, wear, damage

fields.label: Overall Corrosion Assessment
fields.help_text: Assess corrosion level on all structural components

fields.label: Structural Inspection Notes
fields.help_text: Document locations and details of any defects

defect.title: Structural defect found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Structural defect detected that requires evaluation and repair before continued operation.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Inspect all accessible welds for cracks, deterioration, or defects
Check weld quality - look for porosity, undercut, incomplete penetration
Pay special attention to load-bearing and structural welds
Use magnifying glass or dye penetrant for critical welds if available
Any cracks in structural welds are a critical safety issue
Check boom section welds - base, mid, and fly sections
Inspect turret and pedestal welds thoroughly
Check platform attachment welds
Inspect outrigger structural welds

fields.label: All Boom Section Welds
fields.help_text: Inspect all accessible welds on boom structure

fields.label: Any cracks found in any welds?
fields.help_text: Weld cracks are critical - equipment must be tagged out

fields.label: If cracks found, document exact locations
fields.help_text: Precise location description for repair planning

fields.label: Any other weld defects found (porosity, undercut, etc.)?

defect.title: CRITICAL: Weld cracks found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Cracks detected in structural welds. Equipment must be tagged OUT OF SERVICE immediately and evaluated by qualified personnel.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

steps.title: Bolts, Pins, and Fasteners Detailed Inspection

guidance: Assess condition and wear level for each component
When EXCESSIVE wear or other defects are found, click 'Add Defect' to document details
Verify correct grade fasteners are used per manufacturer specifications
Check for proper torque on critical mounting bolts
Verify all pivot pins have proper retaining hardware (cotter pins, snap rings)
Look for elongated holes in mounting points
Include photos for all defects - required for EXCESSIVE wear and SERVICE_REQUIRED severity

fields.label: Structural Bolts (tightness and condition)
fields.help_text: All structural mounting bolts - check for tightness, corrosion, damage

fields.label: Boom Pivot Pins and Retaining Hardware
fields.help_text: Check pins for wear, retainers in place, no play in joint

fields.label: Hydraulic Cylinder Mounting Pins
fields.help_text: All cylinder pin connections - check for wear and proper retention

fields.label: Platform Mounting Bolts and Hardware
fields.help_text: Platform attachment to boom - critical connection point

fields.label: Pedestal Mounting Bolts to Chassis (Critical)
fields.help_text: MOST CRITICAL BOLTS - check torque per specs, look for cracks in flanges

fields.label: Turret Bearing Mounting Bolts
fields.help_text: Critical bearing attachment bolts - proper torque essential

fields.label: Outrigger Bolts and Pins
fields.help_text: All outrigger fasteners and pins - check for wear and tightness

fields.label: Bolts/Fasteners Inspection Notes
fields.help_text: Additional observations, measurements, or general notes about fasteners

defect.title: Critical fastener wear/damage found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Fastener condition requires replacement or repair. See defect details for specific components and recommendations.

standard_text.excerpt: Condition and tightness of bolts and other fasteners.

guidance: Detailed inspection of all hydraulic system components
Check hydraulic pump operation - listen for unusual noise or cavitation
Check all cylinders for leaks, rod scoring, seal condition, and mounting
Inspect all hoses for leaks, chafing, cracks, bulging, age deterioration
Check hose date codes - replace hoses exceeding manufacturer's age limit
Verify relief valve settings per manufacturer specifications
Test holding valves prevent drift under load (critical safety)
Check hydraulic fluid level, condition, and contamination
Inspect hydraulic filters and check service intervals
Check for proper hose routing and protection from chafing

fields.label: Hydraulic Pump (operation, leaks, noise)
fields.help_text: Listen for cavitation or unusual noise

fields.label: Hydraulic Reservoir (level, contamination, leaks)
fields.help_text: Check fluid level and visual condition

fields.label: All Hydraulic Cylinders (leaks, rod damage, mounting)
fields.help_text: Check all boom, rotation, and outrigger cylinders

fields.label: Cylinder Rod Condition (scoring, pitting, chrome damage)
All Hydraulic Hoses (leaks, chafing, age deterioration)
fields.help_text: Check for bulging, cracks, external damage

fields.label: All hoses within manufacturer's age limit
fields.help_text: Check date codes on hoses - typically 10 year max

fields.label: Hydraulic Fittings and Connections
fields.help_text: Check all fittings for leaks and tightness

fields.label: Holding Valves/Load Checks (drift test)
fields.help_text: Critical - test for drift under load

fields.label: Hydraulic Filters (condition and service interval)
fields.help_text: Check bypass indicator and service schedule

defect.title: Hydraulic system defect found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Hydraulic system defect detected that requires repair.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Inspect electrical system components (non-dielectric items)
Check all wiring for damage, proper routing, and protection
Check connectors for corrosion and secure connections
Test all indicator lights, displays, and gauges
Check battery condition, charge level, terminals, and secure mounting
Verify all electrical connections are tight and corrosion-free
Check for any signs of overheating or arcing
Test all work lights and warning lights

fields.label: Wiring Harnesses (damage, chafing, routing)
fields.help_text: Check for damaged insulation or exposed wires

fields.label: Battery (charge, terminals, mounting)
fields.help_text: Battery secure, terminals clean and tight

defect.title: Electrical system defect found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Electrical system defect detected that requires repair.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Test all operating controls from both ground and platform positions
Controls should operate smoothly without binding, jerking, or hesitation
Test boom movements through full range of motion
Check for proper response speed and control precision
Verify controls return to neutral when released
Check simultaneous operation of multiple functions
Listen for unusual noises during operation
Observe smooth, controlled movements in all directions

fields.label: All controls operate smoothly without binding
All controls return to neutral when released

defect.title: Operating control defect found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Operating control malfunction detected. Equipment must not be operated until repairs are completed.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Test all emergency stop buttons (ground and platform)
Verify E-stop immediately halts ALL boom functions
Test ground override/emergency descent controls
Test manual lowering system if equipped
Verify emergency controls are clearly marked, accessible, and functional
All emergency controls must function properly - critical safety systems
Test E-stop reset and verify normal operation resumes

fields.label: Emergency controls clearly marked and accessible

defect.title: CRITICAL: Emergency control defect found
defect.description: Emergency control malfunction detected. Equipment must be tagged OUT OF SERVICE immediately.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Test all safety devices for proper function and calibration
Verify boom angle indicator shows accurate readings
Test load/capacity warning system activates at rated capacity
Test outrigger interlock prevents operation without proper deployment
Check platform gates close and latch securely
Test tilt sensor and warning system if equipped
Verify motion alarms function properly
All safety devices must be functional - no exceptions

defect.title: Safety device defect found during periodic inspection
defect.description: Safety device malfunction detected. Equipment must be tagged OUT OF SERVICE.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

guidance: Verify all required markings, placards, and labels are present and legible
Check capacity chart is current and accurate for equipment configuration
Verify operating manual is present with the equipment
Check manufacturer's serial plate is attached and legible
For insulated units, verify dielectric test placard with date and voltage
Check all warning labels are present and legible
Verify control function labels are clear
Check inspection decal is current

fields.label: Warning Labels (all required labels)
fields.help_text: All required warnings present and legible

fields.label: Dielectric Test Placard (if insulated unit)
fields.help_text: Shows voltage rating and test date

defect.title: Missing or illegible marking/placard/manual found
defect.description: Required marking, placard, or manual is missing or illegible. Must be corrected before continued operation.

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

fields.label: Was load test performed as part of this periodic inspection?
fields.help_text: May be performed separately using Load Test template

fields.label: Load Test Date
fields.help_text: Date of load test if performed separately

fields.label: Load Test Inspection ID or Report Reference
fields.help_text: Reference to separate load test inspection

fields.label: Load Test Notes
fields.help_text: If performed as part of this inspection, reference separate Load Test template for detailed documentation

standard_text.excerpt: Perform a load test of one and one-half times the rated load capacity in accordance with Section 4.5.1 Stability on Level Surface Test.

steps.title: Dielectric Test Documentation (If Applicable and Performed)

fields.label: Does this unit have an insulating boom system?
fields.help_text: Only insulated aerial devices require dielectric testing

fields.label: Is dielectric test required per schedule?
Was dielectric test performed as part of this periodic inspection?
fields.help_text: May be performed separately using Dielectric Test template

fields.label: Dielectric Test Date
fields.help_text: Date of dielectric test if performed separately

fields.label: Dielectric Test Result: PASS
fields.help_text: Dielectric test must pass for insulated units

fields.label: Dielectric Test Inspection ID or Report Reference
fields.help_text: Reference to separate dielectric test inspection

fields.label: Dielectric Test Notes
fields.help_text: If performed as part of this inspection, reference separate Dielectric Test template for detailed documentation

standard_text.excerpt: Insulating aerial devices shall be electrically tested to verify their ability to protect workers from electrical hazards when working near energized conductors.

fields.label: All required items inspected per Section 8.2.4
All operating and emergency controls functional
No structural defects or weld cracks found
Any critical defects found requiring immediate attention?
fields.help_text: Equipment must be tagged out if YES

fields.label: Repairs or adjustments needed before next inspection?
Equipment is safe for continued operation
fields.help_text: Check YES only if all safety requirements met

fields.label: Any operational restrictions or limitations
fields.help_text: Document any restrictions on use or capacity

fields.label: Overall Inspection Summary
fields.help_text: Comprehensive summary of inspection findings, defects found, repairs needed, and recommendations

standard_text.excerpt: An inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed at intervals defined in 8.2.2 depending upon its activity, severity of service, and environment.

rules.title: Equipment must be safe for operation

on_fail.defect_title: Equipment not safe for operation - tag OUT OF SERVICE
on_fail.defect_description: Equipment has failed periodic inspection and must be tagged OUT OF SERVICE until all defects are corrected and equipment is re-inspected per ANSI A92.2-2021 Section 8.2.4.

on_fail.defect_title: Structural defects or weld cracks found
on_fail.defect_description: Structural defects or weld cracks detected. Equipment must be tagged OUT OF SERVICE and evaluated by qualified personnel.
